title: Irenea tricostata
taxonomy: Irena tricostata is a species of plant in the family Mimosaceae. It is a type of mimosa native to Australia. Further classification of Irena tricostata includes being within the genus Irena, which is a small group of flowering plants. The plant is known for its compound leaves and small, white flowers. Its classification is consistent with the broader taxonomy of the angiosperms.

intro: Irena tricostata, also known as the "Australian Mint Bush," is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, toothed leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Irena tricostata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Irena tricostata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and salves to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
